nodes.label: Google Drive Storage
nodes.rationale: Files are uploaded directly to Google Drive via service accounts, bypassing local file persistence limitations on ephemeral deployments like Render or Android packages.

nodes.label: Per-User Data Isolation
nodes.rationale: Records are scoped server-side to their owner (created_by/generated_by); ownership checks return 404 to non-owners so existence is never leaked, ensuring one user's data never appears in another's workspace despite a shared database.

nodes.label: Brevo Transactional Email (HTTP REST API)
nodes.rationale: Production deployments like Render block outbound SMTP ports (587/2525), so email is delivered through Brevo's HTTP REST API instead of SMTP. The REST API key (BREVO_API_KEY, xkeysib-) is a different credential from the SMTP key (SMTP_PASS, xsmtpsib-).

nodes.label: Bloom's Taxonomy (BTL) Generation
nodes.rationale: Uses a Slot-Based Pre-Allocation Strategy (blueprint slots, prompt verb injection, pre-stamping in _finalise) rather than post-generation metadata tagging, which avoids mismatches between a question's content and its BTL label, ensuring 100% taxonomy-content alignment.

nodes.label: LLM Provider Fallback Chain
nodes.rationale: Automatic fallback Groq (Llama-3.3-70b) -> Cerebras (gpt-oss-120b) -> NVIDIA NIM -> Gemini 2.5 Flash ensures maximum uptime. Keys are centrally managed backend environment keys rather than client-supplied, preventing key exposure.

nodes.label: Centralized Server-Side API Key Management
nodes.rationale: Client-side API key and preference stores were removed; custom client-supplied keys are bypassed in favor of centrally managed secure environment keys, preventing key exposure and ensuring consistent configuration.

nodes.label: Admin-Only Account Provisioning
nodes.rationale: Open registration (Register.tsx and /register routes) was removed because only the Admin creates accounts; removing self-registration prevents unauthorized account creation and guarantees integrity of the role-based system.

nodes.label: Chaquopy Java Bridge (chaquopyBridge.ts)
nodes.rationale: Replaces standard axios web requests; the frontend calls a Java Bridge that synchronously invokes the local Python TestClient and returns data natively, embedding the entire Python backend on-device without a centralized web server.

nodes.label: Offline Mode (@capacitor/network)
nodes.rationale: Powered by @capacitor/network; the app blocks access immediately when the device loses internet connection to prevent database crashes.

nodes.label: Deprecated Python Backend (Reference Only)
nodes.rationale: This Python backend folder is NOT used in production; logic was migrated to TypeScript in mobile-app/src/lib. Kept for historical reference and migration documentation.
